data.title: Energy & Utilities Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering Energy and Utilities. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires at 4:20 ET, 12:20 ET and 16:50 ET.

0528 GMT - PTT Exploration and Production's core profit is likely to improve in 2Q, UOB Kay Hian analysts say in a research report, as they raise the stock's target price to THB155.00 from THB143.00 with an unchanged buy rating. An expected nearly 5% on-quarter increase in petroleum sales and gas sale prices staying stable on quarter in 2Q should sufficiently offset a likely fall in oil prices, the analysts say. Based on the brokerage's checks with the Thailand company, its Mozambique liquefied natural gas project remains on track, with production slated to commence in 1H 2029. The brokerage raises its 2025 and 2026 core profit estimates for PTTEP by 8% and 7%, respectively. Shares are 7.7% higher at THB105.00. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

0104 GMT - Santos Chair Keith Spence will seek to calm shareholder nerves at the Australian energy company's annual general meeting, following a 15% drop its in stock price this month. In prepared remarks, Spence says he believes Santos's stock is undervalued, and has been for some time, but that the company is strong and resilient and he is optimistic about its future. Still, "the last week has added a new layer of complexity," he says. "The global trading system that was based on building open, competitive markets over many decades following World War Two" has been upended. Santos is up 4.8% at A$5.595 amid a broadbased rally in global stocks after President Trump decided to pause steep tariffs on nearly 100 nations. (rhiannon.hoyle@wsj.com; @RhiannonHoyle)

0012 GMT - Oil rises in the early Asian session, boosted by President Trump's 90-day pause on higher U.S. tariffs for most countries. Trump's announcement has sparked a risk-on tone across markets, ANZ Research analysts say in a research report. The move has also been "exacerbated by traders being forced to cover some bearish option bets and short positions that had been built over the past few days," the analysts add. Front-month WTI crude oil futures are up 1.0% at $62.97/bbl; front-month Brent crude oil futures are 0.8% higher at $65.98/bbl. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

1926 GMT [Dow Jones]--Crude futures pick up after President Trump pauses some tariffs for 90 days, while raising tariffs on China further following Beijing's retaliation. The tit-for-tat levies between the U.S. and China are "the elephant in the room," Ritterbusch says in a note. The China oil-demand factor "could preclude the energy complex from fully participating in any further near-term increase in global risk appetite," the firm says. "We don't expect this extreme price volatility to subside anytime soon." WTI settles up 4.6% at $62.35, after hitting a four-year low $55.12 earlier. Brent rises 4.2% to $65.48. (anthony.harrup@wsj.com)

1918 GMT [Dow Jones]--U.S. stocks are surging after President Trump says in a social-media post that he authorized a 90-day pause on certain tariffs. Some companies, however, are missing out on the rally. Johnson & Johnson and Church & Dwight, both owners of consumer brands, saw their shares edge lower Wednesday afternoon, as did grocer Kroger, discount retailer Dollar General and fast-food chain McDonald's. Utilities including American Water Works, Consolidated Edison and Duke Energy were also trading in the red, alongside telecommunications companies such as AT&T and Crown Castle. (connor.hart@wsj.com)

1847 GMT [Dow Jones]--Australia has the potential to become a large producer of sustainable aviation fuels, or SAF, according to IFM Investors. One of the country's biggest advantages: About half of the feedstock, including corn and fish oil, used to make such fuels world-wide come from the Asia-Pacific region, the Australian infrastructure-investment firm says. IFM also points to today's limited supply capacity, with 77 SAF refining facilities producing an estimated 502 million gallons last year, or about 0.5% of total aviation-fuel consumption. The firm, which ended December with $74.7 billion in assets, is working with agribusiness company GrainCorp and transport-fuel provider Ampol "on the establishment of an integrated renewable fuels industry in Australia," IFM says. (luis.garcia@wsj.com)

1753 GMT - Crude oil futures rally along with stocks, and U.S. natural gas futures add to gains as President Trump announces a 90-day pause on some tariffs, while racheting up the dispute with China by raising its tariffs to 125%. After hitting a more than four-year low $55.12 early in the session, WTI is up 1.7% at $60.61 a barrel, and Brent rises 1.7% to $63.85 a barrel. Nymex natural gas is up 5.9% at $3.669/mmBtu. (anthony.harrup@wsj.com)

1610 GMT - While the drop in oil prices adds budget pressures to producers in emerging markets such as Saudi Arabia, for the majority of EMs that are importers of oil it could help offset the impact of tariffs, William Jackson of Capital Economics says in a note. "Lower prices provide small but welcome relief in the form of improved terms of trade, lower inflation and a bit more room for central banks to loosen monetary policy." Williams estimates that $60 a barrel oil could reduce emerging market fuel inflation by around 5 percentage points from the firm's baseline assumptions, shaving about 0.3 of a percentage point off overall inflation. Still, many central banks are likely to be cautious given pressure on currencies, he says. (anthony.harrup@wsj.com)

1413 GMT - U.S. natural gas futures are stabilizing after three days of losses, supported by near-term weather forecasts adding some late-season heating demand and strong LNG feedgas flows. Natural gas continues to be caught up in broad market fallout from tariff wars, particularly between the U.S. and China, that has sent crude oil to four-year lows. Also on the downside, the EIA is expected to report an above-average build in natural gas inventories this week, further reducing the storage deficit. Nymex natural gas is off 0.5% at $3.449/mmBtu. (anthony.harrup@wsj.com)

1320 GMT - Oil futures fall to their lowest level in more than four years after China responded to additional U.S. tariffs by increasing its retaliatory tariff to 84%. Escalating trade tensions between the world's two largest economies have raised concerns about a possible recession that would weigh on fuel demand, George Pavel of Naga.com Middle East says in a note. However, the drop in prices could lead to a pullback in output by U.S. shale producers, "particularly as rising production costs, exacerbated by tariffs on steel and equipment, threaten profitability," he adds. WTI is off 4.7% at $56.80 a barrel after falling as low as $55.12. Brent is down 4.6% at $59.92 a barrel. (anthony.harrup@wsj.com)

1248 GMT - A tariff-driven 13% drop in Cheniere Energy's share price looks like a buying opportunity to TD Cowen analysts, who say the new levies are unlikely to result in a pullback in Cheniere's contracted earnings. The trade war probably won't give Cheniere's customers an excuse to avoid paying the fixed fees that make up the overwhelming majority of Cheniere's earnings, the analysts say in a research note. There had been fears that the pandemic would enable some customers to claim force majeure, but even that didn't materialize and the company met its initial 2020 Ebitda guidance, the analysts say. Cheniere Energy is off 3% premarket, and is up 30% over the past 12 months. (dean.seal@wsj.com)

data.title: Basic Materials Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering Basic Materials. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires at 4:20 ET, 12:20 ET and 16:50 ET.

0031 GMT - Peabody could abandon or renegotiate a deal to buy Anglo American's steelmaking coal operations if a fire at one of the mines it is buying is deemed a "material adverse change" under their agreement, Jefferies analyst Christopher LaFemina says in a note. "If it does indeed qualify, it is possible that Peabody could walk away or the two parties could revise the terms of the transaction," says LaFemina. This is a positive for Peabody, and a negative for Anglo American, which could possibly face a new takeover bid from rival BHP if the deal falls apart, he says. "In light of current market conditions and the uncertain market outlook, the consensus view is that Peabody would be best off if it could walk as the risks and the implied valuation are too high," says LaFemina. (rhiannon.hoyle@wsj.com; @RhiannonHoyle)

1748 GMT - Commodity futures across the board are surging after President Trump authorized a 90-day pause on certain tariffs to most countries, except for China. As a result, futures in agriculture, metals, and energy are all surging. Notable examples include copper up 4.7%, lean hogs up 2.3%, gold up 3%, and natural gas up 6%. Earlier today, China announced that it would raise tariffs on American goods to 84%, putting pressure on commodities supported from Chinese buying. (kirk.maltais@wsj.com)

1316 GMT - Economist David Rosenberg says that neither the Treasury market nor USD are playing the role of "safe havens" during this tumult in the global economy. The head of Rosenberg Research warns more mayhem is ahead, as the public has yet to witness the sticker shock at retailers like Walmart, which sources a sizable lot of goods from China. He says a market crash "may be the catalyst to end this insanity." For investors, Rosenberg says the focus should perhaps turn to gold, silver and other commodities. In terms of foreign exchange, he's leaning toward the Swiss franc and Japan's yen.(Paul.Vieira@wsj.com, @paulvieira)

1242 GMT - Market volatility means it is uncertain how wedded Anglo American investors will be to their new Valterra Platinum shares once Anglo American Platinum is demerged from the business and renamed, Berenberg analysts write. Investors understand the strategic logic behind the demerger but spinning off such a major PGM business at the bottom of the commodity cycle leaves value on the table, they add. Anglo American has done well to improve liquidity in the stock via two pre-emptive share offerings, and to temporarily retain a 19.9% shareholding in the business in a move to mitigate share flowback, they add. Anglo American Platinum shares fall 2.65%. (adam.whittaker@wsj.com)

data.title: Auto & Transport Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering the Auto and Transport sector. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires at 4:20 ET, 12:20 ET and 16:50 ET.

0753 GMT - European aerospace and defense stocks jump in early trading after U.S. President Trump announced a 90-day pause for so-called reciprocal tariffs. Among the biggest risers are the U.K.'s Melrose Industries, up 11.5%, and France's Airbus, up 8.9%. In Germany, Rheinmetall shares rise 3.9%, while Italy's Leonardo increases 4.5% and Spain's Indra Sistemas is up 7%. (cristina.gallardo@wsj.com)

0659 GMT - Volkswagen shares will probably benefit from the market relief rally caused by the pause to U.S. reciprocal tariffs, Bernstein analysts write. However, the bank remains skeptical of the group's self-help measures and the efficacy of the VW brand restructuring agreement that was struck before Christmas last year. The company has warned that first-quarter results are materially below consensus. "We are interested to hear how the 600 million euro headwind in the first quarter 2025 from provisions in connection with CO2 regulation in Europe squares with VW impressively doubling the share of BEV in its West European sales to 19%," the analysts say. (dominic.chopping@wsj.com)

0658 GMT - Volkswagen has announced another profit warning, with one-off items impacting earnings by 1.1 billion euros. The warning reduces investor confidence in the underlying earnings, Citi analyst Harald C Hendrikse writes. VW reported first quarter EBIT of 2.8 billion euros from 4.6 billion euros a year ago, versus consensus of 4 billion euros. This includes 1.1 billion euros of exceptional costs. Full-year guidance was maintained, but excludes the tariff impact. Citi expects the news will be overshadowed by U.S. President Trump's 90-day deferral of reciprocal tariffs. However, it says VW is facing too many restructuring, investment, and associate moving parts to be able to accurately forecast quarterly earnings. This severely reduces the value of VW EBIT forecasts for investors, Citi says. (dominic.chopping@wsj.com)

1705 GMT - Delta Air Lines' decision to pull its full-year outlook is understandable, given that visibility is limited in the current demand environment, particularly for its domestic market, Melius Research analysts say in a research note. The Street's 25 EPS estimate for Delta had fallen 23% since the market's peak on Feb 19, the analysts say. With shares rising 7% today, investors seem pleased with Delta's decision to reduce supply to protect margins and cash generation, according to Melius. "Long term, Delta remains much better positioned than most to weather a downturn, which puts them in a position to deepen their competitive moat," the analysts add. (sabela.ojea@wsj.com; @sabelaojeaguix)

1544 GMT - Delta Air Lines will not be paying tariffs on any aircraft deliveries it takes, CEO Ed Bastian says on a call with analysts. The carrier has been clear on that with Airbus, the only plane maker it is getting deliveries from this year, he says. "If you start to put a 20% incremental cost on top of an aircraft, it gets very difficult to make that math work," the CEO says. Delta will work closely with Airbus to address the evolving tariff situation and "do our very best to see what we have to do to minimize tariffs," Bastian says. Delta is hoping tariff-related cost pressures are resolved through trade discussions rather than any actions on the part of either Delta or Airbus, he says. (dean.seal@wsj.com)

1437 GMT - The US and Canada are formally in a tit-for-tat auto trade conflict, with retaliatory, and hefty, Canadian tariffs on US-made vehicles formally kicking in. DesRosiers Automotive Consultants, the Canadian data-research firm, says this is unnecessary from the US end as Canada is a net importer of automotive-related products. For 2024, DesRosiers calculates the US recorded a C$3.2 billion auto trade surplus with its northern neighbor, as a result of sales of heavy-duty trucks, engines and automotive parts. The firm adds 41% of light vehicles sold in Canada were US-made. "The days of Canada being a net exporter in automotive are long gone" says Andrew King, DesRosiers' managing partner. (Paul.Vieira@wsj.com; @paulvieira)

1401 GMT - The recent selloff of Tesla shares looks overblown considering the opportunities the company has coming just around the bend, including the release of a new model in 2Q that could turn around declining vehicle sales, Benchmark analysts say in a research note. They're listing the release of new products as potential catalysts for the EV maker, along with the potential for government contracts, as well as the rollout of full self-driving robotaxis this year. The initial rollout is expected to be limited, so the rate of expansion will be worth watching, the analysts say. They cut Tesla stock's price target to $350 from $475 but maintain a buy rating. Tesla rises 5.6%. (dean.seal@wsj.com)

1332 GMT - Auto-parts maker Continental's expected sale of its ContiTech business is a positive move as the remaining company will be a clean tire asset once the transaction is concluded in 2026, Deutsche Bank analyst Christoph Laskawi writes. While ContiTech has been struggling more recently, cash generation and margin potential especially in the industrial division have been decent overall. The transaction will allow the tire business to reduce debt quickly and move to become a cash return story with a very healthy balance sheet, he adds. "According to management, synergies have been limited between ContiTech and tires which is why there should be close to zero dyssynergies from the disposal." Shares fall 4.1%. (dominic.chopping@wsj.com)

1331 GMT - Rheinmetall reaffirmed its confidence on meeting its full-year guidance during a first-quarter pre-close call, according to Alpha Value analyst Saima Hussain. Management downplayed Rheinmetall's exposure to the U.S., which represents 5% of its revenue, minimizing the risk from tariffs, she says. The German arms maker highlighted the continued strength of its defense business but said it expects its civil segment to remain flat, Hussain adds. Rheinmetall is set to post first-quarter results on May 8. Shares are down 2.6% at 1,282 euros. (cristina.gallardo@wsj.com)

1323 GMT - Continental's management deserve credit for moving aggressively to unlock value for investors, Citi analysts write. They first moved to spin off the automotive unit and have now decided to seek a separation of the ContiTech business. The bank therefore sees Tuesday's three percentage point share price outperformance versus the Stoxx Europe 600 automobiles and parts index as justified. Continental will work to become a high-margin pure-play tire company, Citi adds. The spinoffs should also support a faster deleveraging at the remaining company, which should further the appeal of the equity story, it says. Shares fall 4.1%. (dominic.chopping@wsj.com)

1301 GMT - Mastercard is losing some of its tailwinds as consumer confidence fades, Monness Crespi Hardt analyst Gus Gala says, downgrading it to neutral from buy. The dynamics that have fueled demand for the financial sector are starting to abate, Gala says, and growing uncertainty puts the card issuer at higher risk because it already has a higher return on invested capital. Sagging travel demand reported by Delta Airlines, along with weakening international TSA checkpoint inbounds into the U.S., could also translate to lower earnings for Mastercard, he says. The analyst believes incremental uptake of value-added services or new flows could be slowed as businesses slow down their spending. (katherine.hamilton@wsj.com)

1225 GMT - Delta Air Lines is guiding for sales to either rise 2% or fall 2% in the current quarter, saying potential softness in main cabin sales should be offset by resilient demand for premium travel. In the first three months of the year, premium revenue growth came in at 7% year-over-year while main cabin ticket revenue was down 1%. The company touts its recognition this year by J.D. Power as being the top airline for first-class, business and premium-economy passenger satisfaction. Revenue from loyalty travel awards has been a gainer as well, climbing 11% in 1Q. Shares rise 1.1% to $36.27 premarket. (dean.seal@wsj.com)

data.title: REG - Greatland Gold PLC - March Quarterly Report release date, Investor Call
data.description:
RNS Number : 4975E Greatland Gold PLC 10 April 2025  

Greatland Gold plc (AIM: GGP)

E: info@greatlandgold.com

W: https://greatlandgold.com

: twitter.com/greatlandgold

NEWS RELEASE | 10 April 2025

March Quarterly Report release date,

Investor Conference Call

Greatland Gold plc (AIM:GGP) (Greatland or the Company) will release a Quarterly Activities Report on Monday, 14 April 2025, reporting its first full quarter of production and activities for the period from 1 January 2025 to 31 March 2025.

Shaun Day (Managing Director) and Rowan Krasnoff (Head of Business Development) will host a conference call for shareholders, research analysts and interested stakeholders at 1:00 pm AWST (3.00 pm AEST, 6:00 am BST) on Wednesday, 16 April 2025.

To listen in live, please click on the link below and register your details.

https://webcast.openbriefing.com/greatlandgold-ann-2025/

It is recommended to log on at least five minutes before the commencement time to ensure you are joined in time for the start of the call.

A recording of the call will be available on the same link after the conclusion of the webcast.

Contact

For further information, please contact:

Greatland Gold plc

Shaun Day, Managing Director  |  Rowan Krasnoff, Head of Business Development

info@greatlandgold.com

Nominated Advisor

SPARK Advisory Partners

Andrew Emmott / James Keeshan / Neil Baldwin  |  +44 203 368 3550

Corporate Brokers

Canaccord Genuity  |  James Asensio / George Grainger  |  +44 207 523 8000

SI Capital Limited  |  Nick Emerson / Sam Lomanto  |  +44 148 341 3500

Media Relations

UK - Gracechurch Group  | Harry Chathli / Alexis Gore / Henry Gamble  |  +44 204 582 3500

Australia - Fivemark Partners  |  Michael Vaughan  |  +61 422 602 720

About Greatland

Greatland is a gold and copper mining company listed on the London Stock Exchange's AIM Market and operates its business from Western Australia.

The Greatland portfolio includes the 100% owned Telfer gold-copper mine, the adjacent 100% owned world class Havieron gold-copper project (under development), and a significant exploration portfolio within the surrounding region. The combination of Telfer and Havieron provides for a substantial and long life gold-copper operation in the Paterson Province of Western Australia.

Greatland is targeting a cross-listing on the ASX in the June quarter 2025.

This information is provided by RNS, the news service of the London Stock Exchange. RNS is approved by the Financial Conduct Authority to act as a Primary Information Provider in the United Kingdom. Terms and conditions relating to the use and distribution of this information may apply. For further information, please contact rns@lseg.com or visit www.rns.com.

For further information about how RNS and the London Stock Exchange use the personal data you provide us, please see our Privacy Policy.  END  MSCGPURGCUPAURU

data.title: Propylene prices decrease in Europe
data.description: On Wednesday, propylene prices drifted lower in the European region.An industry source in Europe wishing to remain unidentified informed a Polymerupdate team member,

data.title: A Saudi Arabian producer offers High density polyethylene (HDPE Blow Molding) in Vietnam
data.description: A Polymerupdate source in Vietnam reported that a producer from Saudi Arabia is heard to have offered its HDPE Blow Molding grade at the USD 890/mt levels.This offer is for April 2025 shipment on CFR Ho Chi Minh port basis.

data.title: Eqtec Raises GBP1.5 Million via Subscription Offer
data.description:
Eqtec said Thursday that it raised 1.5 million pounds sterling via a subscription offering with CompactGTL's Compact WTL Tech unit.

The bioscience energy company issued 176,470,588 shares at 0.0085 pound apiece. It also issued 88,235,294 warrants to Compact WTL as part of the offer. Admission of the shares on the London bourse is expected on April 15.

Proceeds were earmarked for the development of Compact GTL and Eqtec's intellectual property for waste-to-liquid technology, working capital, and its purchase of containerized syngas.

Additionally, it amended the terms of its loan deal with YA II PN and Riverfort Global Opportunities, extending its maturity date to Dec. 30, 2027, from May 22, 2026, and dropping its mandatory prepayment duties.

data.title: A producer from Singapore offers Polypropylene (PP Lamination) in Myanmar
data.description: A Polymerupdate source in Myanmar reported that a Singaporean producer is heard to have announced its offer for PP Lamination grade at the USD 1280/mt levels.This offer is for April 2025 shipment on CFR Myanmar Main port basis.

data.title: A producer based in Saudi Arabia offers High density polyethylene (HDPE Blow Molding) in Vietnam
data.description: A Polymerupdate source in Vietnam informed that a Saudi Arabian producer is heard to have announced its offer for HDPE Blow Molding grade at the USD 880/mt levels.This offer is for April 2025 shipment on CFR Ho Chi Minh port basis.

data.title: Smarttech247 Completes Sale of Getvisibility Investment
data.description:
Smarttech247 Group said Thursday it completed the sale of its investment in Visibility Blockchain, d/b/a Getvisibility, to data and cloud security firm Forcepoint.

The cybersecurity company received 1.8 million euros in cash from the sale.

data.title: Euro Rallies as Tariff Shift Tempers ECB Rate Cut Expectations
data.description:
The euro climbed above the $1.10 mark, approaching its strongest level since October 2024, as investors dialed back expectations for European Central Bank rate cuts.

The shift followed an announcement by the Trump administration to temporarily reduce U.S. tariffs in an effort to bring other countries to the negotiating table.

However, U.S. duties on Chinese imports were simultaneously increased to 125% from 104%.

While the move eased fears of a global economic slowdown and surging inflation, it introduced fresh uncertainty that could persist for months, particularly affecting small businesses and consumer confidence in future economic activity.

In response, money markets adjusted their expectations, pricing in an ECB deposit facility rate of 1.8% by December, up from 1.65% on Wednesday and compared to 1.9% the previous week.

The likelihood of an ECB rate cut in April also dropped to 90%, down from full pricing just a day earlier.

data.title: European shares surge after Trump's tariff pause
data.description:
Key points:
  • Tesco warns of profit fall, shares drop 6.9%
  • Euro zone volatility index remains high at 35 points
  • Barry Callebaut shares plummet 23.8% amid cocoa price volatility

European shares surged on Thursday after U.S. President Donald Trump announced an immediate 90-day pause on reciprocal tariffs for most trading partners, prompting a massive relief rally after days-long market rout.

The pan-European STOXX 600 jumped 5.9% at 0752 GMT, after losing 12.5% since the tariffs took effect on April 2 up to last close. Trade-sensitive Germany's benchmark index rose 6.7%.

The suspension of punishing tariffs on dozens of countries came less than 24 hours after they kicked in. Still, the White House maintained a 10% blanket duty on almost all U.S. imports.

European Commission President Ursula von der Leyen welcomed Trump's move a day after the European Union said it would impose 25% tariffs on a range of U.S. imports in the first round of countermeasures, after the country-specific levies took effect.

"This 90-day pause for the tariffs gives time for everyone to negotiate, to find solutions, or in the worst case scenario, reorganize their businesses and prepare to temper the shock. So, that's fundamentally good news," said Ipek Ozkardeskaya, senior market analyst at Swissquote Bank.

"It's also good news to see that Trump could bend, he cannot just push forever."

Meanwhile, Trump further ramped up pressure on China by hiking the tariff on Chinese imports to 125% from 104% that kicked in on Wednesday in retaliation for China's announcement of an 84% levy on U.S. goods starting April 10.

All sectors were higher, with the most battered ones this month - banks (.SX7P), miners (.SXPP) and energy (.SXEP) - advancing 9.4%, 7.5% and 7.2% respectively.

A gauge of euro zone stock market volatility (.V2TX) was still at elevated levels of 35 points, indicating the big swings in markets was far from over.

A violent U.S. Treasury selloff on Wednesday that reignited fears of fragility in the world's biggest bond market also showed some signs of easing on the day.

"With less burdensome extra tariffs and more clarity by mid-year, the US and Europe would suffer a temporary setback to economic growth but would be spared a recession," said Berenberg economist Holger Schmieding.

In company news, Barry Callebaut slumped 23.8% to the bottom of the STOXX index, after the Swiss chocolate maker lowered its annual volume guidance due to what it called "unprecedented volatility" in cocoa bean prices.

Tesco fell 6.9% after Britain's biggest food retailer warned its profit would likely fall this year.

After the U.S. S&P 500 soared 9.5% in its biggest daily gain since 2008 on Wednesday, futures are pointing to a lower open.

data.title: News Highlights: Top Energy News of the Day - Thursday at 4 AM ET
data.description:
Oil Bounces as Trump Holds Off Some Tariffs for 90 Days

Crude futures climbed after President Trump paused some tariffs for 90 days, while raising tariffs on China further following Beijing's retaliation.

U.S. Crude Oil Inventories Rise for Second Straight Week

U.S. crude oil inventories rose for a second consecutive week, climbing 2.6 million barrels, amid an increase in net imports.

Trump Throws Money at Coal. Miner Stocks Love It.

The president signed an executive order designating coal as a "critical mineral."

Peabody Reviews Anglo American Coal Deal After Mine Fire

Peabody Energy said it is reviewing an up to $3.78 billion deal to buy Anglo American's steelmaking coal operations following a fire at an Australian mine.

Energy & Utilities Roundup: Market Talk

Read about sustainable aviation fuels, Cheniere Energy, European oil stocks, Dialog Group, and more in the latest Market Talks covering Energy and Utilities.

The Stock Market Is Bad. The Oil Market Might Be Worse.

Oil prices are down 15% for the year, including a 2.1% fall on Monday.

Shell Lowers Gas Production Guidance

The energy giant lowered its integrated gas production estimate for the first quarter as volumes were hit by unplanned maintenance.

Trump Promised to Lower Energy Prices-but It Wasn't Supposed to Be Like This

The president's tariff blitz sent oil prices tumbling 14% in two days, bludgeoning frackers.

Woodside to Sell 40% Stake in Louisiana LNG Infrastructure

Stonepeak has agreed to invest $5.7 billion in Woodside Energy's proposed Louisiana LNG gas-export infrastructure in the U.S.

Brookfield Buys Colonial Enterprises for $9 Billion

The equity investment in the oil-pipeline firm will be around $500 million, Brookfield said. The agreement includes buying Shell's 16.125% stake in the business.

data.title: Global Markets Leap, Treasury Yields Ease After Trump Pauses Tariffs
data.description:
By Dow Jones Newswires Staff

Global stock markets jumped and Treasury yields eased after President Trump's announcement of a 90-day pause on higher-targeted tariffs for most countries, even as he doubled down on China. Ahead, U.S. CPI will be a focus of investors' attention.

European Union chief Ursula von der Leyen welcomed U.S. President Trump's decision to pause planned tariffs on goods from other countries that sent markets reeling. "It's an important step towards stabilizing the global economy," she said in a statement.

China earlier pledged to accelerate talks with the European Union as Beijing looks to forge closer ties with trading partners.

  • U.S. Treasury yields dropped in early European trading hours, reversing from Wednesday's double-digit increases. The 10-year Treasury yield was recently down 11 basis points at 4.287% and the 30-year Treasury yield was also down 10 basis points, according to Tradeweb data.
  • Equities markets across Asia ended sharply higher and European stock markets leapt at the open, even as U.S. futures eased following Wall Street's rally. South Korea's benchmark Kospi rose 6.6% to close at 2445.06, Australia's S&P/ASX 200 closed 4.5% higher, bolting to its highest gain since March 2020. Chinese shares ended higher in anticipation of stimulus measures to be announced soon despite Trump's increase to U.S. tariffs on China to 125%.
  • In Europe, the Stoxx Europe 600 gained 6.9% in morning trading, the FTSE 100 added 5.5%, France's CAC 40 increased 6.8% and Germany's DAX climbed 7.3%. Bank, technology and pharmaceutical shares were notable risers early in the European trading day.
  • The dollar fell with the remaining tariffs still likely to be a significant hit to the U.S. economy. The DXY dollar index against a basket of major currencies was recently down 0.2% to 102.644.
  • Oil prices retreated after a brief rally. Benchmark Brent crude fell 1.4% to $64.58 a barrel, having settled 4% higher on Wednesday.
  • Gold futures rose 1.6% at $3,127.20 a troy ounce, nearing the all-time record of $3,201.60/oz, set in early April.

Write to Barcelona Editors at barcelonaeditors@dowjones.com

data.title: Norion Bank’s results for the first quarter of 2025 exceeds market expectations
data.description:
(Figures within parentheses refer to the first quarter of 2024)

Norion Bank AB (“Norion Bank”) does not recognize interest income from loans where borrowers have been placed in Stage 3 until actual payments have been received. Such payments amounting to approximately SEK 140 million, which are attributable to Norion Bank’s Real Estate segment, have been received during the first quarter of 2025.

- I am pleased that our real estate clients, who historically have experienced payment challenges and therefore been placed in Stage 3, now have been able to pay their accrued interest, says Martin Nossman, CEO, Norion Bank.

Total income is estimated to amount to approximately SEK 1,010-1,020 million (933) and operating profit to approximately SEK 510-520 million (417) for the first quarter of 2025.

The report for the first quarter of 2025 will be published on 24 April 2025.

data.title: Acconeer receives order within level measurement worth USD 125 000
data.description:
The order comes from Micro Summit K.K. and is intended for level sensing in tanks with a major Japanese module maker. The order relates to the A121 pulsed coherent radar sensor for mass production.

CEO Ted Hansson comments: “It is great to see a large order in level measurement, which is one of our focus areas and an important market for us. The fact that we get volume orders in our focus areas shows that our go-to-market strategy is starting to give results.”

In addition to publicly announced orders, Acconeer continuously receives orders of smaller amounts which are not publicly announced as they are not considered to affect the share price.

data.title: Copper Holds Most of Rebound
data.description:
Copper futures in the US held above the $4.3 per pound mark on Thursday, retaining most of its rebound since hitting three-month lows two sessions prior after US President Trump paused his reciprocal tariff package for 90 days.

The reciprocal tariff package had initially excluded copper, but brought levies on non-retaliating countries to 10% and eased recession fears in the world's largest economy, improving broad expectations of manufacturing demand.

Still, copper futures are over 20% down from their record high of $5.3 touched on March 26th as Trump signaled he will still deliver tariffs specifically targeted at copper in upcoming weeks.

The threat widened the premium of copper futures in the US against comparable contracts in the LME due to the stress that trade barriers would place on limited US copper smelting capacity.

Also, the trade war with China was escalated to higher tariffs from both parties, pressuring the steady flow of copper scrap from the US to China.

data.title: China Meets With EU to Bolster Trading Ties Amid US Reciprocal Tariffs
data.description:
China seeks to strengthen its trading ties with the European Union as both sides expressed their willingness to resume a trade remedy dialogue amid the US' introduction of reciprocal tariffs, the Chinese Commerce Ministry said in a Thursday press release.

Commerce Minister Wang Wentao met with European Commissioner for Trade and Economic Security Maros Sefcovic in a video call on Tuesday.

Wang called the US President Donald Trump's reciprocal tariffs as a "typical act of unilateralism, protectionism and economic bullying", while Sefcovic said the tariffs affected international trade.

Sefcovic also said that the EU is willing to work with other members of the World Trade Organization, as well as China, to ensure the normal operation of international trade, the press release said.

data.title: Khoobsurat Gets License to Establish Beer Manufacturing Unit in Goa, India
data.description:
Khoobsurat has received license approval from the State Government of Goa, India for the establishment and start of operations at its' Goa unit, according to a Thursday filing to the BSE.

The company will manufacture and bottle beer in collaboration with Salecte Brewing at the Goa unit.

data.title: Coca-Cola Europacific Partners plc Announces Notice of AGM
data.description:
UXBRIDGE, ENGLAND / ACCESS Newswire / April 10, 2025 / Coca-Cola Europacific Partners plc ("CCEP") announces that the Notice of Meeting for its 2025 Annual General Meeting ("Notice of AGM") is available to view at: https://www.cocacolaep.com/about-us/governance/shareholder-meetings in which CCEP reaffirms its comparable operating profit guidance for the year ending 31 December 2025, as set out in its full year results announced on 14 February 2025.

The AGM is to be to be held at 3:30pm BST on 22 May 2025, at Pemberton House, Bakers Road, Uxbridge, UB8 1EZ.

CCEP's 2024 Annual Report and Form 20-F ("2024 Annual Report") was published on 21 March 2025 and can be found at https://ir.cocacolaep.com/financial-reports-and-results/annual-reports

The 2024 Annual Report, Notice of AGM and Form of Proxy are also being sent to those shareholders who have requested to receive hard copies.

In compliance with Listing Rule 6.4.1R, the Notice of AGM and Form of Proxy will shortly be available for inspection on the National Storage Mechanism at: https://data.fca.org.uk/#/nsm/nationalstoragemechanism.

CCEP's Q1 2025 trading update will be announced on 29 April 2025.

CONTACTS

  • Company Secretariat

    Clare Wardle

    clare.wardle@ccep.com

  • Investor Relations

    Sarah Willett

    sarah.willett@ccep.com

  • Media Relations

    Shanna Wendt

    mediaenquiries@ccep.com

ABOUT CCEP

Coca-Cola Europacific Partners is one of the world's leading consumer goods companies. We make, move and sell some of the world's most loved brands - serving nearly 600 million consumers and helping over 4 million customers across 31 countries grow.

We combine the strength and scale of a large, multi-national business with an expert, local knowledge of the customers we serve and communities we support.

The Company is currently listed on Euronext Amsterdam, NASDAQ, London Stock Exchange and on the Spanish Stock Exchanges, and a constituent of both the Nasdaq 100 and FTSE 100 indices, trading under the symbol CCEP.

For more information about CCEP, please visit www.cocacolaep.com and follow CCEP on LinkedIn

This information is provided by RNS, the news service of the London Stock Exchange. RNS is approved by the Financial Conduct Authority to act as a Primary Information Provider in the United Kingdom. Terms and conditions relating to the use and distribution of this information may apply. For further information, please contact rns@lseg.com or visit www.rns.com.

SOURCE: Coca-Cola Europacific Partners plc

data.title: Eight SEE countries get respite as Trump pauses tariffs above 10%
data.description:
BELGRADE (Serbia), April 10 (SeeNews) - U.S. President Donald Trump said he was pausing for 90 days reciprocal tariffs above the baseline 10% rate on importers into the country - a move that grants a reprieve to eight countries of Southeast Europe.

More than 75 states have reached out to the White House to engage in trade negotiations and have not retaliated "in any way, shape, or form" against the U.S., Trump wrote in a post on Truth Social on Wednesday, less than a day after the higher tariffs took effect.

In Southeast Europe, Serbia, Bosnia and Herzegovina, North Macedonia, and Moldova were set to face reciprocal tariffs exceeding the standard 10% rate - of 37%, 36%, 33% and 31%, respectively, having been flagged for having significant barriers to imports from the U.S.

Albania, Kosovo and Montenegro are subject to the 10% baseline rate. European Union members Bulgaria, Romania, Slovenia, and Croatia were planned to be levied 20% tariffs, which were set to apply to the entire bloc.

The 10% baseline tariff became effective last Sunday, while the levies above that rate took effect at midnight on Tuesday.

Trump, however, said he would raise the tariff on Chinese imports to 125% from the previously announced 104%, "based on the lack of respect that China has shown to the World's Markets."

The U.S. announced reciprocal tariffs last week, looking to tackle its trade deficit that amounted to $1.2 trillion (1.1 trillion euro) in 2024.

($ = 0.909 euro)

data.title: Bulgaria Industrial Output Slips For 3rd Month
data.description:
Industrial production in Bulgaria dropped by 4.6% year-on-year in February 2025, slipping further from a downwardly revised 3.3% fall in the previous month.

This marked the third consecutive month of decline in industrial activity and the steepest in the sequence, mainly due to a sharp decrease in manufacturing output (-7.1% vs -0.3%).

Meanwhile, production recovered significantly for electricity, gas, steam & air-conditioning supply (11.2% vs -13.6%), while it declined less for mining and quarrying (-1.8% vs -4.5%).

On a seasonally adjusted monthly basis, industrial activity went down by 0.7% in February, slowing from an upwardly revised 2.1% loss in the preceding period.
